---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 2: Image Formation and Physics of Data
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---


## 01. Title: Image Formation and Physics of Data

- Unit role in course flow.

## 02. Learning objectives

- Outcomes for lecture + exercise.

## 03. Why physics of data matters for ML

- Garbage-in mechanisms.

## 04. Recap from Unit 1

- Trustworthy baseline logic.

## 05. Measurement chain overview

- Sample -> instrument -> signal -> data.

## 06. Contrast mechanisms basics

- Signal origin intuition.

## 07. Resolution and point spread

- Information limits.

## 08. Sampling theorem intuition

- Nyquist and aliasing.

## 09. Noise taxonomy

- Shot/readout/thermal examples.

## 10. Heteroscedastic noise in experiments

- Non-uniform uncertainty.

## 11. Drift and temporal instability

- Practical acquisition issues.

## 12. Calibration and normalization

- Comparable data creation.

## 13. Artifact taxonomy (images)

- Blur, charging, stripes, etc.

## 14. Artifact taxonomy (spectra)

- Baseline, peak shifts, saturation.

## 15. Preprocessing goal definition

- What should be preserved.

## 16. Denoising trade-offs

- Signal preservation vs smoothing.

## 17. Filtering in spatial/frequency domains

- Conceptual comparison.

## 18. Fourier intuition for microscopy data

- Frequency content view.

## 19. Windowing and boundary effects

- Hidden assumptions.

## 20. Segmentation prerequisites

- Label quality dependence.

## 21. Annotation uncertainty

- Inter-rater variability.

## 22. Class imbalance consequences

- Metric distortions.

## 23. Feature extraction pathways

- Hand-crafted vs learned.

## 24. Leakage through preprocessing

- Pipeline anti-patterns.

## 25. Train/val/test under instrument grouping

- Split strategy importance.

## 26. Temporal split for process data

- Causality-aware validation.

## 27. Metric selection by task

- MAE/F1/AUROC calibration links.

## 28. Calibration of confidence outputs

- Reliability of probabilities.

## 29. Uncertainty-aware decision thresholding

- Reject option motivation.

## 30. Case sketch: defect detection

- Pipeline scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: spectrum classification

- Pipeline scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: process monitoring

- Time-series caution.

## 33. Failure mode #1

- Model learns artifact instead of material signal.

## 34. Failure mode #2

- Domain shift across instruments.

## 35. Failure mode #3

- Label leakage from metadata.

## 36. Mitigation checklist

- Practical safeguards.

## 37. Physics-informed feature ideas

- Domain priors in baseline models.

## 38. Data augmentation caveats

- Physically valid transforms only.

## 39. Reproducibility checklist

- Versioning and provenance.

## 40. Documentation template

- What students should report.

## 41. MFML dependency map

- Concepts reused from MFML.

## 42. Link to Unit 3 (data quality + ML readiness)

- Forward transition.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: inspect raw data

- Identify artifacts.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: preprocessing A/B

- Compare outcomes.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: baseline model

- Train and evaluate.

## 46. Exercise scaffold: split comparison

- Random vs grouped/temporal.

## 47. Exercise scaffold: failure analysis

- One concrete post-mortem.

## 48. Exam-oriented key statements

- High-yield review.

## 49. Summary

- What to retain.

## 50. References + reading assignment

- Next unit bridge.

::: {#refs}
:::
